decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and evaluates an inference framework estimating reproduction number and transmission heterogeneity from identical-sequence cluster sizes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and evaluation of a transmission-parameter inference framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses matched payment and testing microdata to estimate retail-store transmission and its contribution to the reproduction number.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete SARS-CoV-2 transmission analysis in Danish retail stores.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a genomic-surveillance method for inferring variant fitness gains directly from nucleotide-frequency changes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a new genomic method for inferring a transmission-related fitness quantity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Assesses bias in transmissibility estimates caused by assuming constant serial-interval distributions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly evaluates bias in Rt inference and proposes time-varying serial-interval quantification.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates generation time and time-varying and effective reproduction numbers for monkeypox virus clade I.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete transmission-parameter inference from historical mpox outbreak data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces EpiFusion for joint inference of Rt from phylodynamic and epidemiological data using particle filtering.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicitly proposes, validates, benchmarks, and applies a new Rt-inference method.
supported_label_variants: joint inference of the effective reproduction number

decisions.relationship_evidence: Measures social contacts and age mixing but does not infer transmission dynamics or transmission parameters.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The abstract provides contact-pattern evidence, not a lineage source for transmission-dynamics inference.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses case pairs and a catalytic model to estimate generation time, transmission patterns, susceptibility, and regional effective reproduction numbers.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete measles transmission inference using Italian surveillance data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Estimates household secondary attack rates, serial intervals, and R0 in a prospective mpox cohort.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete household transmission analysis of clade Ib mpox in Burundi.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits a compartmental transmission and observation model to ST131 clades and estimates clone-specific R0 values.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete inference of Escherichia coli clone transmissibility.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an SIR model with exponential-growth and maximum-likelihood methods to estimate generation time and R0.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete transmission analysis of recombinant African swine fever virus in pigs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reconstructs dengue transmission chains with a Bayesian framework and estimates generation time, Rt, transmission distances, and intervention effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete inference of DENV-2 transmission during Italian outbreaks.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits an age-structured measles transmission model to 172 seroprevalence studies to estimate setting-specific R0.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multicountry measles transmissibility analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a transmission model to estimate reproduction numbers, underreporting levels, and underreporting-adjusted incidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Kala-azar transmission analysis incorporating underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Combines surveillance and serological data in mathematical and statistical models to estimate reporting rates, incidence, and transmissibility.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete seasonal-influenza model accounting for surveillance under-reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Models dengue dynamics and contrasts simulated infections with reported cases to infer substantial underreporting and subclinical infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete dengue transmission model with inferred underascertainment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Constructs a pertussis transmission model and estimates age-specific under-reporting by comparing projected incidence with confirmed cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete pertussis model explicitly estimating under-recognized burden.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits a deterministic SEIR transmission model to both raw and underreporting-corrected Ebola case data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete Ebola transmission analysis using corrected and uncorrected surveillance series.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses combined stochastic incidence and imported-case models to estimate transmission parameters and the case-reporting rate.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete MERS-CoV transmission model explicitly accounting for missed links and underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents a novel multilayer dynamic HCV transmission model explicitly distinguishing diagnosed and undiagnosed infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of a transmission model incorporating underreporting and diagnostic status.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Models early epidemic growth and uses maximum likelihood to estimate unreported cases, reporting change, and R0.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete early 2019-nCoV analysis centered on under-reporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Explains how underreporting and imperfect diagnostics make zero reported gHAT cases an unreliable proxy for elimination of transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Synthesizes prior modeling and explicitly recommends priorities for future transmission modeling and measurement.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses time-dependent mathematical models and uncertainty quantification to estimate COVID-19 underreporting and transmission dynamics across three countries.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete comparative COVID-19 modeling application incorporating underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops an inference approach that controls for underreporting and changing surveillance intensity when fitting transmission models.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological approach for transmission inference under observation confounding.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Studies SEIRD identifiability and demonstrates downward R0 bias and distorted forecasts when case underreporting is ignored.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance and bias analysis of epidemic inference under underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces a Bayesian SIR approach with an explicit correction for under-reported COVID-19 cases.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of an underreporting-corrected transmission model.
supported_label_variants: SIR model with correction for under-reporting

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a new SI(Q/F)RD state-space model incorporating a time-varying fraction of undetected infections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit new transmission-model extension for underreporting.
supported_label_variants: state-space SIR model to account for Underreporting

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents PolSIRD with an observation mechanism for under-reporting and intervention-dependent transmission parameters.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and evaluation of an underreporting-aware epidemic model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a two-strain transmission framework, but the abstract only mentions an under-reporting context and does not describe an underascertainment mechanism.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit lineage contribution for transmission models that model underascertainment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Builds and estimates a 92-country behavioral transmission model that quantifies cases and deaths relative to official reports.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multinational COVID-19 model estimating underascertainment.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses mathematical epidemic models to predict underreported infections and deaths from Nepalese surveillance data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 underreporting application in a limited-data setting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Updates a mechanistic gHAT transmission model with refinements to observed-case variance, underreporting, and passive-screening improvements.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete refined transmission analysis for the Mandoul focus.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops an excess-mortality framework to reconstruct SARS-CoV-2 transmission and quantify underreporting of infections and deaths.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological framework for inference when routine case and death reporting is incomplete.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Reconstructs epidemic equations from African case and death series and estimates substantial country-level underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete multicountry COVID-19 dynamics analysis incorporating underestimation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a compartmental Zika model with mosquito and sexual transmission and fits it to estimate under-reporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a transmission model designed to approximate underreporting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits an age-structured SEIRS model that integrates age- and period-specific underdetection parameters from seroprevalence studies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete German SARS-CoV-2 transmission model accounting for underdetection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces a new SEIR-type model and physics-informed neural-network approach that incorporates under-reporting of cases and deaths.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development for underreporting-aware epidemic inference.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Introduces a novel SIRDS model with fuzzy epidemic-period transitions to estimate time-varying parameters and case underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit new underreporting-aware transmission model with validation and forecasting analyses.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops a data-driven SEIR model with an inferred change-point separating pre- and post-intervention transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development of a change-point epidemic model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes a pseudo-likelihood change-point growth model for fitting multiple epidemic waves under misspecification.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological source proposing a change-point model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Fits a piecewise-constant transmission rate through changepoint analysis to produce weekly ICU-admission projections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete COVID-19 policy-modeling application of transmission-rate changepoints.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops Bayesian stochastic-filtering methods to detect unknown changepoints in an SIR transmission rate.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological treatment of Bayesian changepoint detection in epidemic models.
supported_label_variants: Bayesian changepoint detection for epidemic models

decisions.relationship_evidence: Presents an iterated-filtering framework for inferring piecewise-constant transmission-rate changepoints.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development of a changepoint-detection algorithm for compartmental epidemic models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a clinical and economic microsimulation alongside a transmission model but does not identify the simulation as agent-based or individual-based.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Microsimulation alone is not explicit evidence for the exact agent-based-model concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an agent-based model to explain influenza spread across three Canadian communities.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete historical-influenza application of agent-based modeling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Constructs an individual-based synthetic-society model, Little Italy, to generate and validate contact matrices.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and validation of an individual-based model using artificial agents.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a Markov microsimulation of HIV clinical outcomes without explicitly describing an agent-based or individual-based model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit agent-based-method lineage contribution.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Builds an agent-based model to simulate and compare echinococcosis control strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete parasitic-disease control application of an ABM.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Proposes and describes the implementation of a spatially explicit, multilevel agent-based schistosomiasis transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and implementation of an agent-based transmission framework.
supported_label_variants: spatially explicit agent-based schistosomiasis transmission model

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and validates an individual-based model combining influenza ecology, epidemiology, and evolution.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development and validation of a novel IBM framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the MISCAN microsimulation screening model but does not identify it as agent-based or individual-based.
decisions.lineage_evidence: No explicit agent-based-model relationship or lineage lead.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses an established microsimulation model without explicit agent-based or individual-based characterization.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Microsimulation terminology alone does not establish the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses STDSIM, explicitly described as an individual-based microsimulation model, to project HIV-vaccine impact.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HIV-vaccination application of an individual-based model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Evaluates influenza weekend-extension interventions using an established spatially explicit agent-based simulation model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete influenza-control application of agent-based simulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Applies an Individual Space-Time Activity-based Model to simulate influenza control measures.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete influenza intervention application of an individual-based simulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops an agent-based framework representing patients and healthcare workers to simulate emergency-department influenza spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit development and software implementation of an agent-based disease-spread framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses the SIMCOLEP microsimulation but does not explicitly identify it as an agent-based or individual-based model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Uses a microsimulation of structured populations but does not explicitly name an agent-based or individual-based design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Develops and simulates an agent-based farm model of the full Toxoplasma gondii transmission cycle.
